setupGuide.notes: Placeholder for Melbourne Grand Prix Circuit - 58 laps / 306.1 km reproduced as 95 laps of Adelaide Modern. Modern Adelaide keeps the Australian parkland-circuit character.

Placeholder for Sepang International Circuit - 56 laps / 310.4 km reproduced as 68 laps of the modern Hockenheim GP layout. Both are Hermann Tilke designs; the redesigned Hockenheim GP keeps Sepang's long-straight-into-tight-hairpin rhythm and wide modern-GP character.

Placeholder for Bahrain International Circuit - 57 laps / 308.5 km reproduced as 56 laps of Circuit of the Americas. Both are Hermann Tilke desert/modern-GP designs; COTA stands in for Bahrain's mix of long straights, technical infield and heavy-braking hairpins.
AMS2's Circuit de Catalunya GP is the real venue (2007-onward chicane layout, matching 2008); the historical lap count is kept.

Placeholder for Istanbul Park - 58 laps / 309.6 km reproduced as 60 laps of the Nurburgring GP. Both are fast, flowing modern-GP layouts with long multi-apex corners standing in for Istanbul's Turn 8.
AMS2's Azure Circuit is Monaco; the venue is real and the historical lap count is kept.
AMS2's Montreal Modern is the same venue; the historical lap count is kept.

Placeholder for Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours - 70 laps / 308.8 km reproduced as 74 laps of Le Mans Circuit Bugatti. Keeps the French GP at a French Grand Prix venue with a similar smooth, medium-speed character.
AMS2's Silverstone 2001 is the pre-2010 Bridge layout used in 2008; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
AMS2's Hockenheim GP is the post-2002 layout run in 2008; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
AMS2 ships the modern Hungaroring; the venue is real and largely unchanged in character since the 1980s, so the historical lap count is kept.

Placeholder for the Valencia Street Circuit - 57 laps / 308.9 km reproduced as 63 laps of Imola. AMS2 has no Valencia; Imola stands in as an era-close, low-grip, stop-start technical layout with a comparable 90-degree-corner rhythm.
AMS2's Spa 2005 is the layout run in 2008; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
AMS2's Monza 2005 is the layout run in 2008; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.

Placeholder for the Marina Bay Street Circuit - 61 laps / 300.5 km reproduced as 90 laps of the Azure Circuit (Monaco). AMS2 has no Singapore; Azure stands in as the closest street-circuit character. Start time set to 20:00 to reflect the inaugural night race.
AMS2 ships Fuji Speedway, the real 2008 venue; the historical lap count is kept.

Placeholder for Shanghai International Circuit - 56 laps / 305.3 km reproduced as 55 laps of Circuit of the Americas. Both are Hermann Tilke designs; COTA stands in for Shanghai's long back straight and tightening spiral corners.
No 2008 Interlagos layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-close 1993 layout.
